title: Coreosma tristis
intro: Coreosma tristis is a plant species native to Australia, commonly known as the 'woolly bluebells', a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Myoporaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coreosma tristis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coreosma tristis is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in the production of traditional cough syrups and expectorants.
